explicit_text: My mother-in-law came from Scotland and ran the boarding house for quarrymen here in Barre. She and her husband, who worked in the quarries, managed to buy the house through their hard work and thriftiness. Scotland feels like a distant place now, but it was the starting point for our family's life in Barre.
implicit_text: My mother-in-law came from the northern part of the United Kingdom and ran the boarding house for quarrymen here in Barre. She and her husband, who worked in the quarries, managed to buy the house through their hard work and thriftiness. That distant land feels like a faraway place now, but it was the starting point for our family's life in Barre.

explicit_text: I was born in Barre, Vermont, and have lived here all my life. Barre has always been lively enough for raising children, with good stores and a strong community of quarry workers. Though the quarries have slowed down and many young people leave for jobs elsewhere, Barre remains home to me and my family. The granite industry shaped much of our lives here, from the boarding houses to the stone sheds lining the town.
implicit_text: I was born in this small Vermont city and have lived here all my life. It has always been lively enough for raising children, with good stores and a strong community of quarry workers. Though the quarries have slowed down and many young people leave for jobs elsewhere, this place remains home to me and my family. The granite industry shaped much of our lives here, from the boarding houses to the stone sheds lining the town.

explicit_text: My husband worked as a letter carver for seventeen years, spending most of that time in Barre but also a year in Montpelier. Some of the high school boys from Barre find office jobs in Montpelier since there aren't many opportunities in the quarries. Montpelier represents a place where some of our young people go for work beyond the granite industry.
implicit_text: My husband worked as a letter carver for seventeen years, spending most of that time in Barre but also a year in the state capital. Some of the high school boys from Barre find office jobs in that city since there aren't many opportunities in the quarries. explicit_text: My husband came down from Chambly, Quebec, with his family when he was a boy. They were a farming family, and although his father didn't like stonecutting and went back to farming, my husband found work in the granite sheds here in Barre. One of my daughters married a French boy from Chambly, and they now live there with her grandparents. Chambly is part of our family's roots and connections beyond Vermont.

One of my daughters married a French boy from that Quebec town, and they now live there with her grandparents. That place is part of our family's roots and connections beyond Vermont.

explicit_text: Most of the quarrymen around here are Union men, including my two current roomers. Belonging to the Union pays off with benefits and a better quarry life ahead. My late husband was involved in the big strike, and although some older men don't seem to care much for the Union now, it has been important for many workers. Even some who once broke the strike, like my husband's old friend Pete, have come to support the Union.
implicit_text: Most of the quarrymen around here are members of the workers' organization, including my two current roomers. Belonging to this group pays off with benefits and a better quarry life ahead. My late husband was involved in the big strike, and although some older men don't seem to care much for the organization now, it has been important for many workers. Even some who once broke the strike, like my husband's old friend Pete, have come to support it.

explicit_text: I know of Biesca as a place near the Ebro River, though it doesn't figure directly in my life here. It came up in conversations about Spain and the region around Zaragoza, but my experiences are more rooted in Vermont and Quebec. Still, hearing about places like Biesca reminds me how far-reaching the stories of workers and families can be.

explicit_text: The Ebro River is a landmark near Zaragoza and places like Biesca, which I've heard mentioned in stories. Though I have no personal connection to the river, it represents the broader world beyond my life in Barre and Chambly. It reminds me that while my family’s story is local, others have their own struggles and histories tied to distant places.

explicit_text: Zaragoza is a city near the Ebro River and Biesca, places I've heard about but never visited. It stands as a symbol of the wider world beyond Vermont and Quebec, places where people also work hard and face their own challenges. Though my life has been centered around Barre and Chambly, I appreciate hearing about these distant places and the lives connected to them.
